cards.text: Article 15A is the citizenship pathway for foundlings and stateless children — and the government decides each case without published criteria.
cards.sub: There is no statutory checklist for what makes an application succeed. Civil-society casework shows decisions taking years and outcomes varying widely.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.h: Article 15A grants federal government discretion to register minors as citizens — by design, not by oversight failure.
cards.s: The discretion was constitutional from the start. The transparency question is whether the criteria for exercising it should be published.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.h: The 2024 Constitution (Amendment) Bill resolved the maternal-citizenship gap for overseas-born children — but did not address the broader Article 15A discretion.
cards.s: Pre-amendment cases were closed via the March 2025 Federal Court consent order in Suriani Kempe v Kerajaan Malaysia.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.h: For children waiting on 15A decisions, the wait itself is the harm: no identity documents, no school enrolment, no healthcare access.
cards.s: Family Frontiers and other advocacy groups have documented multi-year cases where the wait outlasts the child's primary-school years.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.text: Citizenship granted by undocumented discretion is not citizenship — it is favour. The fix is publishing criteria, not just amending the constitution.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

cards.text: After the 2024 maternal-citizenship amendment, Article 15A discretion is the next reform: published criteria, statutory deadlines, and disaggregated outcome reporting.
sources: BACKFILL: traced from currently-published cards. Pre-pipeline issue; primary sources to be added in research brief expansion.

issue_headline: Article 15A Citizenship Applications Are Decided Without Published Criteria — Many Wait Years
